mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth indicates robust economic demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high-GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or GDP growth fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production signals improved supply-side efficiency and corporate capacity utilization, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or INDPRO fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market (low unemployment) combined with high interest rates creates inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in scenarios with UNRATE < 4% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effects vanish in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent core inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-CPI/low-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effects fail to materialize in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and stock prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high-RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or RSAFS fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals robust job market conditions, which increase consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high-PAYEMS environments are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or PAYEMS fails to predict returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong demand for goods and services, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-HOUST/RSAFS environments are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effects fail to materialize in out-of-sample tests.
